CIV_1548.family_context: likely C IV doublet member; compare with sibling transition as supporting evidence, not as a hard filter

CIV_1550.family_context: likely C IV doublet member; compare with sibling transition as supporting evidence, not as a hard filter

soft_background.summary: C IV is a likely doublet: similar velocity features across CIV_1548 and CIV_1550 support the absorber interpretation.
soft_background.expected_relation: For an unsaturated clean absorber, the stronger 1548 member is usually deeper than the 1550 member at similar velocity; saturated lines can approach a 1:1 depth ratio.
soft_background.outlier_policy: Do not discard single-member or inconsistent features automatically. Preserve and report them as possible blends, contamination, bad pixels, low S/N, saturation, edge effects, or ambiguous real cases.
soft_background.agent_guidance: Use sibling agreement as evidence, not a hard rule. If only one member shows a feature, keep it visible and report the mismatch rather than deleting it by default.

soft_background.summary: H I Ly-alpha is a single transition in this catalog entry.
soft_background.expected_relation: There is no doublet sibling panel for direct same-ion confirmation in this local view.
soft_background.outlier_policy: Preserve unusual residuals and report possible blends, contamination, bad pixels, low S/N, saturation, or ambiguous real cases.
soft_background.agent_guidance: Do not require sibling support for H I Ly-alpha; use local profile shape, masks, continuum, and external context.
